title: Fix the Simple Array Index Generator

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function `generateIndices` that takes an array of integers as input and returns a new array containing the indices of the non-zero elements in the input array. The function should be efficient and handle large input arrays.

examples.explanation: Indices of non-zero elements in the input array
No non-zero elements in the input array
Indices of all elements in the input array

hints.text: Use the Array.prototype.filter() method to filter out zero elements

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The function should use a simple and efficient approach to generate the indices.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve manually checking each element and its index.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the Array.prototype.map() method to generate the indices.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the Array.prototype.map() method in combination with the Array.prototype.filter() method.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return the correct indices for all test cases.
editorial.dryRun: Let's try running the function with the test case [1, 0, 2, 0, 3].
